headline: France's Iranian community torn between republicans and monarchists
description: Many Iranians in France hope for regime change back home. However, those in favor of democracy are losing ground to supporters of a return to the monarchy, led by the son of Iran's former shah.

headline: Netanyahu vows to carry on war, 'eradicate Iranian regime'
description: Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu said that thanks to the bombing campaign by Israeli and American pilots, 'We have gained almost complete control of the airspace' over Iran's capital. Speaking to the Iranian people, he said the 'moment of truth' was coming as Israel was seeking to help liberate them from the 'yoke of tyranny.'

headline: Kuwait airport, Saudi Arabia targeted as Iran presses Gulf attacks
description: Fuel tanks at Kuwait's international airport were targeted in a drone attack, the country's military said, calling the strike 'a direct targeting of vital infrastructure.' Saudi Arabia's defense ministry said Sunday it had intercepted and destroyed 15 drones that entered the kingdom's airspace, including six east of the capital Riyadh.

headline: Cultural heritage under threat from Middle East war
description: Several sites, including Tehran's Golestan Palace and Tel Aviv's Bauhaus buildings, have sustained damaged from missile strikes.

headline: In Siwa, the spartan luxury of an ecolodge in the Egyptian desert
description: On the border between Egypt and Libya, Siwa is only accessible after a night bus ride from Cairo. Although tourism is growing there, the oasis remains an unspoiled destination. For the past 30 years, the Adrère Amellal hotel, built from clay and salt, has been welcoming visitors at the foot of a sculptural mountain.

Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
description: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.
byline: Alexandre Horn,  Asia Balluffier and  Ghazal Golshiri

headline: Azerbaijan and Armenia, both neighbors of Iran, fear regional spillover of conflict
description: Two Iranian drones struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan region on Thursday, raising fears of an expansion of hostilities in the South Caucasus, as Baku maintains close ties with Israel.

headline: War in Iran puts political pressure on both isolationist Vance and interventionist Rubio
description: Neither the US vice president nor the secretary of state stands to gain from a show of military force that lacks a clear strategy and runs counter to the MAGA doctrine. The conflict is primarily Donald Trump's, who appears unconcerned about his political legacy.
